message:
fix(cli): render Toon output format errors as JSON and close beads issue

Treat OutputFormat::Toon the same as Json when deciding whether to
render errors as structured JSON, with two new tests covering
command-level and env-level Toon error output.  Close the
beads_rust-cdpi tracking issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -328,7 +328,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-c58","title":"Export Error Policies","description":"## Overview
Implement configurable error handling policies for JSONL export. Different use cases require different trade-offs between strictness and resilience.

## Error Policy Enum
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
pub enum ExportErrorPolicy {
    /// Abort export on any error (default for sync)
    #[default]
    Strict,
    
    /// Skip problematic records, export what we can
    BestEffort,
    
    /// Export valid records, report failures (for debugging)
    Partial,
    
    /// Only export issues (skip deps/labels/comments on error)
    RequiredCore,
}
```

## Technical Requirements

### Policy Implementation
```rust
pub struct ExportContext {
    pub policy: ExportErrorPolicy,
    pub errors: Vec<ExportError>,
    pub warnings: Vec<String>,
}

impl ExportContext {
    pub fn handle_error(&mut self, err: ExportError) -> Result<(), ExportError> {
        match self.policy {
            ExportErrorPolicy::Strict => {
                // Fail immediately
                Err(err)
            }
            ExportErrorPolicy::BestEffort => {
                // Log and continue
                tracing::warn!("Export error (skipping): {}", err);
                self.errors.push(err);
                Ok(())
            }
            ExportErrorPolicy::Partial => {
                // Record for report, continue
                self.errors.push(err);
                Ok(())
            }
            ExportErrorPolicy::RequiredCore => {
                // Fail only if its a core (issue) error
                match err.entity_type {
                    EntityType::Issue => Err(err),
                    _ => {
                        self.errors.push(err);
                        Ok(())
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Export With Policy
```rust
impl SqliteStorage {
    pub fn export_jsonl_with_policy(
        &self,
        output_dir: &Path,
        policy: ExportErrorPolicy,
    ) -> Result<ExportReport> {
        let mut ctx = ExportContext::new(policy);
        let mut report = ExportReport::new();
        
        // Export issues
        let issues = self.get_all_issues()?;
        let issues_path = output_dir.join("issues.jsonl");
        let mut issues_file = File::create(&issues_path)?;
        
        for issue in &issues {
            match serde_json::to_string(issue) {
                Ok(json) => {
                    writeln!(issues_file, "{}", json)?;
                    report.issues_exported += 1;
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    ctx.handle_error(ExportError {
                        entity_type: EntityType::Issue,
                        entity_id: issue.id.clone(),
                        message: e.to_string(),
                    })?;
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Export dependencies
        let deps = self.get_all_dependencies()?;
        // ... similar pattern
        
        // Export labels
        let labels = self.get_all_labels()?;
        // ... similar pattern
        
        // Export comments
        let comments = self.get_all_comments()?;
        // ... similar pattern
        
        report.errors = ctx.errors;
        report.policy_used = policy;
        Ok(report)
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# Default: strict (fail on any error)
br sync --flush-only

# Best effort: export what we can
br sync --flush-only --error-policy best-effort

# Partial: full report of failures
br sync --flush-only --error-policy partial

# Required core: issues must succeed, others can fail
br sync --flush-only --error-policy required-core
```

### Export Report
```rust
pub struct ExportReport {
    pub issues_exported: usize,
    pub dependencies_exported: usize,
    pub labels_exported: usize,
    pub comments_exported: usize,
    pub errors: Vec<ExportError>,
    pub policy_used: ExportErrorPolicy,
}

pub struct ExportError {
    pub entity_type: EntityType,
    pub entity_id: String,
    pub message: String,
}

impl ExportReport {
    pub fn has_errors(&self) -> bool {
        !self.errors.is_empty()
    }
    
    pub fn success_rate(&self) -> f64 {
        let total = self.issues_exported + self.dependencies_exported 
                  + self.labels_exported + self.comments_exported;
        let failed = self.errors.len();
        if total + failed == 0 { 1.0 }
        else { total as f64 / (total + failed) as f64 }
    }
}
```

### Output (Human)
```
Export completed with policy: best-effort

Exported:
  156 issues
  234 dependencies (2 errors)
  89 labels
  42 comments (1 error)

Errors (3):
  dependency bd-abc12 -> bd-xyz99: Invalid reference
  dependency bd-def34 -> bd-missing: Target not found
  comment c-123: Serialization failed
```

### Output (JSON)
```json
{
  "policy": "best-effort",
  "exported": {
    "issues": 156,
    "dependencies": 234,
    "labels": 89,
    "comments": 42
  },
  "errors": [
    { "type": "dependency", "id": "bd-abc12", "message": "Invalid reference" }
  ],
  "success_rate": 0.98
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Strict policy fails on first error
- [ ] BestEffort skips errors, continues export
- [ ] Partial exports valid records, reports all failures
- [ ] RequiredCore fails only on issue errors
- [ ] --error-policy CLI flag
- [ ] ExportReport includes error list
- [ ] Human-readable error summary
- [ ] JSON error report

## Unit Tests
- Strict policy aborts on error
- BestEffort continues after error
- Partial collects all errors
- RequiredCore fails on issue error
- RequiredCore succeeds despite dep error
- Error count accurate
- Success rate calculated correctly
- Each entity type triggers correct behavior

## Dependencies
- JSONL Export Implementation
- Model Types
- Error Handling Module

## Rationale
Different scenarios need different error tolerance. Sync should be strict to prevent silent data loss. Debugging needs partial export to identify problematic records. Backup needs best-effort to export as much as possible despite corruption.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:23:50.298979959Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:43:24.051726823Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T18:43:24.051726823Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-c7yg","title":"E2E tests: audit command","description":"# E2E Tests for `audit` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br audit record <event>` - Record interaction
- `br audit list` - List audit events
- `br audit --json` - JSON output

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Record single event, verify in list
2. Record multiple events, verify order
3. Audit list with date filters
4. JSON output structure validation

### Error Cases
5. Audit before init → error
6. Record with empty event → error

### Edge Cases
7. Very long event text
8. Event with special characters
9. Concurrent audit recording
10. Audit log rotation (if implemented)

 {"id":"beads_rust-ciu","title":"JSONL Export Implementation","description":"# JSONL Export Implementation

## Purpose
Implement classic export semantics: atomic JSONL write, include tombstones, exclude ephemerals, safety guard against empty DB overwrites, and metadata updates.

## Export Rules
- Include **tombstones**.
- Exclude ephemerals/wisps (`ephemeral=true` or ID contains `-wisp-`).
- Sort by ID for deterministic output.
- Populate dependencies/labels/comments for each issue.

## Atomic Write
- Write to temp file in same directory → fsync → rename.
- Default permissions: 0600 (single-repo). Auto-flush may set 0644.

## Safety Guard
- Refuse to overwrite **non-empty JSONL** if DB has **zero issues** unless `--force`.

## Metadata Updates (after success)
- Clear dirty flags for exported IDs.
- Update `jsonl_content_hash` + `last_import_time`.
- Touch DB mtime to be ≥ JSONL mtime.

## Error Policies (config)
- `strict` (default), `best-effort`, `partial`, `required-core`.
- Optional `.manifest.json` with failures/warnings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Atomic write + deterministic ordering.
- Safety guard enforced.
- Metadata updates applied.

## Tests
- Export of empty DB with existing JSONL (guard).
- Deterministic ordering.
- Manifest output when enabled.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:32:20.260358724Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T16:37:02.581077346Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T16:37:02.581077346Z","close_reason":"JSONL export complete. Fixed create_issue() ephemeral/pinned/is_template bug.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-clp","title":"Scope guardrails: non-invasive boundaries + classic-only command set","description":"# Scope Guardrails (Non-Invasive + Classic-Only)

## Purpose
Lock in **non-invasive** boundaries and the **classic** command set. This bead is the scope contract for br v1.

## Required Scope (Classic v1)
- Core CRUD: `init`, `create` (incl. `--file`), `update` (incl. bulk + `--claim`), `close`, `reopen`, `delete`.
- Views: `list`, `show`, `ready`, `blocked`, `search`, `stats/status`, `count`, `stale`, `orphans`.
- Relations: `dep` (add/remove/list/tree/cycles), `label` (add/remove/list/list-all), `comments` (add/list).
- Scheduling: `defer`, `undefer`.
- Sync: `sync --flush-only`, `sync --import-only` (NO git ops).
- Config: YAML + DB config (`config` command), metadata.json.
- Support modes: `--no-db` JSONL-only.

Optional-but-documented (post-core):
- `where`, `info`, `version`, `q`, `lint`, `graph`, `epic`.
- Port-plan extras: saved queries, CSV export, changelog, local history backups.

## Explicit Exclusions (MUST NOT SHIP in v1)
- Daemon/RPC or background services.
- Git hooks, merge drivers, auto-commit/push/pull, sync-branch worktrees.
- Linear/Jira integrations, mail delegation.
- Gastown features (agent/molecule/gate/rig/convoy/HOP).
- Git-touching maintenance (reset/restore/repair/cleanup auto-fix).

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI help reflects included commands only.
- Excluded features absent from CLI and docs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:03:15.959423160Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:57:07.966311461Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:57:07.966311461Z","close_reason":"Scope guardrails acknowledged and adhered to","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-cmi","title":"CSV export format (list/export)","description":"# CSV Export Format (list/export)

## Purpose
Add CSV output for list/export for non-technical consumers (plan enhancement).

## CLI
- `br list --format=csv`
- `br export --format=csv`
- Optional `--fields` to select columns (default set below).

## Default Fields
`id,title,status,priority,issue_type,assignee,created_at,updated_at`

## Behavior
- Use a CSV writer with proper escaping.
- `--fields` can include description/notes; multi-line fields are quoted.
- Preserve deterministic ordering (same as list/export).

## Acceptance Criteria
- CSV output has header row.
- Correct field ordering and escaping.
- Works with filters (list/export).

## Tests
- CSV with commas/newlines in fields.
- Field selection.","notes":"Added E2E CSV list coverage in tests/e2e_list_priority.rs (header/escaping + --fields newline quoting). cargo test list_csv passes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CopperMeadow","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:26.967295200Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:37:40.500774613Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:37:40.500774613Z","close_reason":"CSV formatting implemented in src/format/csv.rs and list --format=csv in src/cli/commands/list.rs; E2E list CSV tests noted in issue","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-rt7z","title":"Integrate rich output into quick commands (q, reopen, defer, undefer, count)","description":"## Commands: br q, br reopen, br defer, br undefer, br count

### Traffic Level: LOW-MEDIUM
Quick action commands that need simple confirmations.

### br q (quick create)
```
✓ Created beads_rust-abc1
  "Quick note about something"
```

### br reopen <ID>
```
✓ Reopened beads_rust-abc1
  Status: closed → open
```

### br defer <ID> [UNTIL]
```
✓ Deferred beads_rust-abc1
  Status: open → deferred
  Until: 2024-02-01 (7 days)
```

### br undefer <ID>
```
✓ Undeferred beads_rust-abc1
  Status: deferred → open
```

### br count [--status STATUS]
```
╭─ Issue Counts ──────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  Total: 156                                         │
│  Open: 89    In Progress: 34    Closed: 33          │
│  Ready: 23   Blocked: 12   Deferred: 5              │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Location: tests/cli/quick_tests.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_q_creates_issue() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_q_result("Quick note", &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("Created"));
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_shows_status_change() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_reopen_result("test-id", &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("closed"));
    assert!(output.contains("open"));
}

#[test]
fn test_defer_shows_until_date() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let until = chrono::Utc::now() + chrono::Duration::days(7);
    let output = render_defer_result("test-id", until, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("deferred") || output.contains("Until"));
}

#[test]
fn test_count_json_unchanged() {
    let current = run_count_json();
    let baseline = load_fixture("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/count.json");
    assert_json_eq!(current, baseline);
}
```

### E2E Test Script
Location: tests/e2e/quick_e2e.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Quick Commands ==="
setup_test_db

# Test q (quick create)
log_step "Testing br q"
Q_OUTPUT=$(br q "Quick task" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$Q_OUTPUT" "Created"
Q_ID=$(echo "$Q_OUTPUT" | grep -oE 'beads_rust-[a-z0-9]+' | head -1)
log_pass "br q works"

# Test count
log_step "Testing br count"
COUNT_OUTPUT=$(br count 2>&1)
assert_contains "$COUNT_OUTPUT" "Total"
log_pass "br count works"

# Test defer
log_step "Testing br defer"
DEFER_OUTPUT=$(br defer "$Q_ID" "2099-01-01" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$DEFER_OUTPUT" "deferred"
log_pass "br defer works"

# Test undefer
log_step "Testing br undefer"
UNDEFER_OUTPUT=$(br undefer "$Q_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$UNDEFER_OUTPUT" "open"
log_pass "br undefer works"

# Close and test reopen
br close "$Q_ID" 2>&1 > /dev/null
log_step "Testing br reopen"
REOPEN_OUTPUT=$(br reopen "$Q_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$REOPEN_OUTPUT" "Reopened"
log_pass "br reopen works"

log_success "=== Quick commands E2E: ALL PASSED ==="
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log quick command type and result
- Log status changes for reopen/defer/undefer
- Log rendering mode","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:37:16.000283475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T20:00:19.949044682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T20:00:19.948988286Z","close_reason":"Rich output integrated for q, reopen, defer, undefer, count commands. Files pass rustfmt validation. Full cargo check blocked by unrelated error in audit.rs (smart quotes) - notified TealCat.","created_at":"2026-01-20T20:00:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rxg","title":"Config system (YAML + DB precedence + metadata.json)","description":"# Config System (YAML + DB + metadata.json)

## Purpose
Implement classic bd configuration semantics: **YAML config files + env vars + DB config table**, plus `.beads/metadata.json` for startup file paths. This is foundational for correct prefix handling, auto-import/flush flags, and routing behavior.

## Sources & Precedence (highest wins)
1. **CLI flags**
2. **Environment variables** (`BD_*`, plus select `BEADS_*`)
3. **Project config** `.beads/config.yaml` (walk up from CWD)
4. **User config** `~/.config/bd/config.yaml`
5. **Legacy user config** `~/.beads/config.yaml`
6. **DB config table** (runtime keys)
7. **Defaults**

## YAML-only Keys (startup settings)
Must live in config.yaml (cannot be stored in DB):
- `no-db`, `no-daemon`, `no-auto-flush`, `no-auto-import`, `json`
- `db`, `actor`, `identity`
- `flush-debounce`, `lock-timeout`, `remote-sync-interval`
- `git.*`, `no-git-ops`, `no-push` (read-only in br)
- `sync-branch` / `sync.branch` (ignored in br v1)
- `routing.*`, `directory.labels`, `external_projects`, `validation.*`, `hierarchy.max-depth`

## DB Config Keys (classic)
- `issue_prefix`
- `allowed_prefixes`
- `status.custom`, `types.custom`
- `import.missing_parents`
- `export.error_policy`, `auto_export.error_policy`, `export.retry_attempts`, `export.retry_backoff_ms`, `export.skip_encoding_errors`, `export.write_manifest`
- `max_collision_prob`, `min_hash_length`, `max_hash_length`

## metadata.json (startup file config)
File: `.beads/metadata.json`
Fields:
- `database` (DB filename, default `beads.db`)
- `jsonl_export` (JSONL filename, default `issues.jsonl`)
- `backend` (ignore dolt in br)
- `deletions_retention_days` (legacy, ignore)

If missing, use defaults. Legacy `config.json` should be auto-migrated to `metadata.json` on init (optional).

## Env Bindings
- `BD_*` (dots/hyphens => underscores)
- Legacy: `BEADS_FLUSH_DEBOUNCE`, `BEADS_AUTO_START_DAEMON`, `BEADS_IDENTITY`, `BEADS_REMOTE_SYNC_INTERVAL`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correct precedence order for all config sources.
- YAML-only keys never stored in DB.
- metadata.json controls DB/JSONL paths before DB open.
- Env overrides honored.

## Tests
- Precedence tests (env overrides YAML overrides DB).
- metadata.json path override tests.
- YAML-only key handling tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:13.657995776Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:26:32.599293076Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T17:26:32.599293076Z","close_reason":"Implemented config precedence, startup resolution, metadata path handling, lock-timeout support, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-s68c","title":"Integrate rich output into show command","description":"## Command: br show <ID>

### Traffic Level: HIGH
The detail view command - used when agents or humans need full issue context.

### Current Implementation
Location: src/cli/commands/show.rs
Output: Multi-line formatted text with key: value pairs

### Integration Steps
1. Import OutputContext and IssuePanel from output module
2. Replace manual formatting with IssuePanel::new()
3. Add description rendering (may contain markdown)
4. Add comments section if requested
5. Add dependency tree section

### Visual Result
```
╭─ beads_rust-abc1 ──────────────────────────────────╮

```

### Multi-Issue Display
When showing multiple issues (br show ID1 ID2 ID3), render panels sequentially with a blank line separator.

### Backward Compatibility
- --json outputs full issue object (unchanged)
- Text mode maintains same information density
- Exit codes unchanged

---

```rust
#[test]
fn test_show_uses_issue_panel_component() {
    // Verify show command uses IssuePanel for rendering
    let issue = create_test_issue();
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let panel = IssuePanel::new(&issue, &ctx);
    assert!(panel.render().contains(&issue.title));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_json_output_unchanged() {
    // Compare JSON output with baseline
    let current = run_show_json("test-id");
    let baseline = load_fixture("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/show.json");
    assert_json_eq!(current, baseline);
}

#[test]
fn test_show_renders_dependencies() {
    // Verify dependency section appears for issues with deps
    let issue = create_issue_with_deps();
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let output = render_show(&issue, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("Blocked by"));
    assert!(output.contains("Blocks"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_renders_comments() {
    // Verify comments section appears when issue has comments
    let issue = create_issue_with_comments();
    let output = render_show(&issue, &OutputContext::rich());
    assert!(output.contains("Comments"));
}

#[test]
fn test_show_multiple_issues_separated() {
    // Showing multiple issues should have blank line separation
    let issues = vec![create_test_issue(), create_test_issue()];
    let output = render_show_multiple(&issues, &OutputContext::rich());
    assert!(output.contains("\n\n")); // Double newline separator
}
```

### E2E Test Script
Location: tests/e2e/show_e2e.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Show Command ==="

# Setup
setup_test_db
TEST_ID=$(create_test_issue_with_all_fields)

# Test 1: Basic show command
log_step "Testing basic show command"
OUTPUT=$(br show "$TEST_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$OUTPUT" "$TEST_ID" "Should show issue ID"
assert_contains "$OUTPUT" "Status" "Should show status field"
assert_contains "$OUTPUT" "Priority" "Should show priority field"
log_pass "Basic show works"

# Test 2: JSON output backward compatibility
log_step "Testing JSON backward compatibility"
CURRENT=$(br show "$TEST_ID" --json | jq -S .)
# Verify structure matches expected schema
assert_json_has_key "$CURRENT" "id"
assert_json_has_key "$CURRENT" "title"
assert_json_has_key "$CURRENT" "status"
log_pass "JSON structure correct"

# Test 3: Show with dependencies
log_step "Testing show with dependencies"
DEP_ID=$(create_issue_with_dependencies)
DEP_OUTPUT=$(br show "$DEP_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$DEP_OUTPUT" "Blocked by" "Should show blockers"
log_pass "Dependencies displayed"

# Test 4: Show multiple issues
log_step "Testing multi-issue show"
MULTI_OUTPUT=$(br show "$TEST_ID" "$DEP_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$MULTI_OUTPUT" "$TEST_ID" "Should show first issue"
assert_contains "$MULTI_OUTPUT" "$DEP_ID" "Should show second issue"
log_pass "Multiple issues work"

# Test 5: Rich mode panel rendering
log_step "Testing rich mode panel"
RICH_OUTPUT=$(script -q /dev/null br show "$TEST_ID" 2>&1 || true)
# Rich mode should have box drawing characters
assert_contains "$RICH_OUTPUT" "─" "Should have box drawing in rich mode"
log_pass "Rich panel rendering works"

log_success "=== Show command E2E: ALL PASSED ==="
```

 {"id":"beads_rust-s9a","title":"Output formats & JSON schema parity (IssueWithCounts/Details/Blocked/TreeNode)","description":"# Output Formats + JSON Schema Parity

## Purpose
Ensure text and JSON outputs match classic bd shapes, including IssueWithCounts/IssueDetails/BlockedIssue/TreeNode and legacy JSON error behavior.

## Core JSON Shapes
- **IssueWithCounts**: Issue fields + `dependency_count`, `dependent_count`.
- **IssueDetails**: Issue fields + `labels`, `dependencies`, `dependents`, `comments`, `parent`.
- **BlockedIssue**: Issue fields + `blocked_by_count`, `blocked_by` (array of IDs).
- **TreeNode**: Issue fields + `depth`, `parent_id`, `truncated`.

## Command JSON Expectations
- `list` / `search`: array of IssueWithCounts.
- `show`: array of IssueDetails (even for single ID).
- `ready`: array of Issue.
- `blocked`: array of BlockedIssue.
- `dep tree`: array of TreeNode.
- `count`: `{count}` or `{total, groups}`.
- `stats/status`: StatusOutput summary.

## Text Output Key Points
- Status icons: ○ open, ◐ in_progress, ● blocked, ❄ deferred, ✓ closed, ✗ tombstone, 📌 pinned.
- List default ordering: priority ASC, created_at DESC.
- Pretty/tree formatting uses dependency tree connectors (`├──`, `└──`).
- Agent mode (if implemented) strips emoji/colors and uses `ID: Title`.

## JSON Error Behavior (legacy quirks)
- Some fatal errors emit `{ "error": "..." }` to **stdout**.
- Others emit JSON error to **stderr**.
- Some commands still print text errors even with `--json`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON shapes match bd for classic commands.
- Text output matches golden snapshots (see snapshot bead).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:03:24.175642669Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:54:07.999179359Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:54:07.999179359Z","close_reason":"Implemented output formats module with JSON types (IssueWithCounts, IssueDetails, IssueWithDependencyMetadata, BlockedIssue, TreeNode, Statistics) and text formatting functions (format_status_icon, format_priority, format_type_badge, format_issue_line) with status icon constants. All 10 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-sav","title":"prime Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br prime` command to preload context for AI coding agents. This outputs structured information about ready issues, blocked work, and project state in a format optimized for LLM consumption.

## CLI Interface
```
br prime [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --limit <N>               Max issues to include (default: 10)
  --focus <ID>              Focus on specific issue and its context
  --include-closed          Include recently closed issues
  --json                    Output as JSON (default)
  --markdown                Output as Markdown
```

## Technical Requirements

### Context Assembly
```rust
pub struct AgentContext {
    pub ready_issues: Vec<IssueWithCounts>,
    pub in_progress: Vec<IssueWithCounts>,
    pub recently_closed: Vec<IssueWithCounts>,
    pub blocked_summary: BlockedSummary,
    pub project_stats: ProjectStats,
    pub focus_issue: Option<IssueDetails>,
}

fn assemble_agent_context(opts: &PrimeOptions) -> Result<AgentContext> {
    let storage = open_storage()?;
    
    let ready = storage.get_ready_issues(opts.limit)?;
    let in_progress = storage.list_issues(&ListFilter {
        status: Some(Status::InProgress),
        limit: Some(opts.limit),
        ..Default::default()
    })?;
    
    let recently_closed = if opts.include_closed {
        storage.list_issues(&ListFilter {
            status: Some(Status::Closed),
            since: Some(days_ago(7)),
            limit: Some(5),
            ..Default::default()
        })?
    } else {
        vec![]
    };
    
    let blocked = storage.get_blocked_summary()?;
    let stats = storage.get_project_stats()?;
    
    let focus = opts.focus.as_ref()
        .map(|id| storage.get_issue_details(id))
        .transpose()?;
    
    Ok(AgentContext { ready, in_progress, recently_closed, blocked, stats, focus })
}
```

### Markdown Output Format
```markdown
# Beads Context

## Project Stats
- Total: 156 issues (89 open, 12 in progress, 55 closed)
- Blocked: 41 issues waiting on dependencies
- Velocity: 1.14 issues/day (last 7 days)

## Ready to Work (10 issues)
| ID | Priority | Type | Title |
|----|----------|------|-------|
| bd-abc12 | P0 | bug | Fix authentication timeout |
| bd-def34 | P1 | feature | Add user preferences |
...

## In Progress (2 issues)
| ID | Priority | Title |
|----|----------|-------|
| bd-ghi56 | P1 | Implement caching layer |

## Blocked Summary
- 41 issues blocked
- Top blockers: bd-xyz99 (blocks 5), bd-abc12 (blocks 3)

## Focus: bd-abc12 (if --focus provided)
**Fix authentication timeout**
Type: bug | Priority: P0 | Status: open

### Description
The authentication system times out after 30 seconds...

### Blocked By
- bd-xyz99: Database connection pooling

### Blocking
- bd-def34: Add user preferences (waiting for auth fix)
```

## Use Cases

### Agent Session Start
```bash
# Agent reads context at session start
br prime --limit 5 --json > /tmp/context.json
```

### Focus on Specific Issue
```bash
# Agent working on specific issue
br prime --focus bd-abc12
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Output ready issues (unblocked work)
- [ ] Output in-progress issues (current work)
- [ ] Output project statistics
- [ ] Output blocked summary
- [ ] --focus includes full issue details and dependencies
- [ ] --include-closed shows recent completions
- [ ] JSON output (default)
- [ ] Markdown output
- [ ] Configurable limit

## Unit Tests
- Empty project returns valid (empty) context
- Ready issues sorted by priority
- In-progress issues included
- Focus issue includes dependencies
- Recently closed filtered by date
- Markdown format is valid
- JSON format is valid

## Dependencies
- ready Command (get_ready_issues)
- blocked Command (blocked summary)
- stats Command (project stats)
- show Command (issue details)
- SQLite Storage Layer Core

## Rationale
AI coding agents need structured context to work effectively. The prime command provides this in a consistent format, reducing the need for agents to run multiple commands to understand project state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:19:29.104863452Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:49:17.702231946Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:49:17.702231946Z","close_reason":"Out of scope for br classic parity (prime command not supported)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-w1i","title":"lint Command Implementation","description":"# lint Command (template validation)

## Purpose
Read-only validation of required template headings in issue descriptions. This is classic bd behavior (substring match, case-insensitive), not a strict Markdown parser.

## CLI
```
br lint [issue-id...] [--type <type>] [--status <status>]
```
Defaults:
- If no IDs: lint open issues only.
- Daemon mode excluded; direct storage only.

## Required Sections (classic)
- **bug**: "Steps to Reproduce" + "Acceptance Criteria"
- **task/feature**: "Acceptance Criteria"
- **epic**: "Success Criteria"
- Other types: no requirements

Matching rules:
- Case-insensitive substring search.
- Markdown prefixes (`#`, `##`) stripped before matching.

## JSON Output
```json
{
  "total": 3,
  "issues": 2,
  "results": [
    { "id": "bd-abc", "title": "Fix", "type": "bug", "missing": ["## Steps to Reproduce"], "warnings": 1 }
  ]
}
```
Notes:
- `results` includes only issues with warnings.
- JSON mode exits 0 even with warnings.
- Human mode exits 1 if warnings exist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Required headings matched case-insensitively.
- JSON and human outputs match bd.

## Tests
- Issue descriptions with and without required headings.
- Exit code differences between JSON and human modes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:50.930149167Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:36:25.244001818Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:36:25.244001818Z","close_reason":"Lint command fully implemented: template section validation for bug/task/feature/epic types with case-insensitive matching, JSON output support, exit codes per spec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-w5c","title":"Local history backups (.br_history) + history command","description":"# Local History Backups (.br_history) + history Command

## Purpose
Provide a local safety net for JSONL exports by snapshotting `issues.jsonl` into `.beads/.br_history/` on every export. This is a **new feature** from the port plan (not in bd) and must be optional + configurable.

## Design
- Directory: `.beads/.br_history/` (gitignored)
- Snapshot naming: `issues.YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM-SS.jsonl` (filesystem-safe ISO)
- On export:
  - If current `issues.jsonl` exists, copy to history before writing new file.
  - Skip backup if content hash matches most recent snapshot.

## Rotation Policy (configurable)
- `history.enabled` (default true)
- `history.max_count` (default 100)
- `history.max_age_days` (default 30)

## CLI
```
br history list
br history diff <snapshot>
br history restore <snapshot>
br history prune --keep <N> --older-than <days>
```
- `restore` imports snapshot into DB and re-exports.
- History is local-only; never committed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Backups created on export (manual or auto-flush).
- Rotation respects count + age.
- `history list/diff/restore/prune` work and log clearly.

## Tests
- Export creates snapshot; repeated export with same content does not.
- Prune removes oldest snapshots and respects age cutoff.
- Restore imports correctly and re-exports JSONL.","notes":"ASSESSMENT (2026-01-17): History feature is SUBSTANTIALLY IMPLEMENTED.

✅ IMPLEMENTED:
- Automatic timestamped backups on export (.br_history)
- Rotation respects count + age (HistoryConfig)
- history list - works after path fix (beads_rust-1rvm)
- history diff <snapshot> - works
- history restore <snapshot> - works
- history prune --keep <N> --older-than <days> - works

BUG FIXED (beads_rust-1rvm):
